perturbation.original_text: 1.2 "Products" means the item listed in the Schedule and any additional products expressly brought within the scope of this agreement by mutual written consent of the parties. 1.3 "Performance Levels" means the annual minimum sales of the Products for the Sales Territory as set forth in the Schedule or as may be amended by the parties in writing from time to time. 1.4 "Sales Price" means, for Products delivered to the ANUBIS hereunder, an amount in US Dollars, determined from SCAM's current applicable price list for such Product as published by SCAM, in effect September 21, 2005 as mutually agreed by both parties, and which shall only be changed with 30 days prior written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 "Products" means the item listed in the Schedule and any additional goods expressly brought within the scope of this agreement by mutual written consent of the parties. 1.3 "Performance Levels" means the annual minimum sales of the items for the Sales Territory as set forth in the Schedule or as may be amended by the parties in writing from time to time. 1.4 "Sales Price" means, for items delivered to the ANUBIS hereunder, an amount in US Dollars, determined from SCAM's current applicable price list for such Product as published by SCAM, in effect September 21, 2005 as mutually agreed by both parties, and which shall only be changed with 30 days prior written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation replaces 'Products' with 'goods' and 'items.' However, the definition itself retains 'Product' when referencing SCAM's price list. This creates confusion as to whether 'goods', 'items' and 'Products' are interchangeable and if the price list applies to all three, particularly concerning the price changes with 30 days prior written notice, contradicting the term 'Product' referenced later in Article 1.4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.4 "Sales Price" means, for items delivered to the ANUBIS hereunder, an amount in US Dollars, determined from SCAM's current applicable price list for such Product as published by SCAM, in effect September 21, 2005 as mutually agreed by both parties, and which shall only be changed with 30 days prior written notice.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Products' with 'goods' and 'items' in the definition section (1.2 and 1.3), but the 'Sales Price' definition (1.4) retains 'Product' when referencing SCAM's price list. The modified text causes an in-text contradiction as the term 'Product' is no longer consistently defined, leading to uncertainty as to whether 'goods', 'items' and 'Products' are interchangeable. Since 'Sales Price' depends on 'Product', it is unclear what the price list applies to, thereby creating ambiguity and a clear contradiction. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition of in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1 EXTENT OF REPRESENTATION RIGHTS. Under the terms and conditions set forth in this agreement, SCAM hereby appoints ANUBIS, and ANUBIS accepts such appointment as the representative to sell the Products to customers in the Sales territory. Unless specifically otherwise authorized in writing by SCAM, ANUBIS shall not directly or indirectly by any means whatsoever contact or solicit any customer or customers outside of Sales Territory or establish any branch or depot for the purpose of selling the Products outside of the Sales Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 EXTENT OF REPRESENTATION RIGHTS. Under the terms and conditions set forth in this agreement, SCAM hereby appoints ANUBIS, and ANUBIS accepts such appointment as the marketing agent to sell the Products to patrons in the Sales territory. Unless specifically otherwise authorized in writing by SCAM, ANUBIS shall not directly or indirectly by any means whatsoever contact or solicit any consumer or consumers outside of Sales Territory or establish any branch or depot for the purpose of selling the Products outside of the Sales Territory.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'representative' and 'customers' have been replaced with 'marketing agent' and 'patrons/consumers', respectively. If 'marketing agent' and 'representative' have different meanings, it changes ANUBIS's powers and obligations. Also, the use of 'patrons/consumers' (plural), creates uncertainty around targeting, contradicting any marketing strategies that are to be implemented.
perturbation.contradicted_text: To diligently use it best efforts to introduce, promote the sale of, and obtain orders for, the Products in the Sales Territory.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'representative' to 'marketing agent' introduces ambiguity, as the scope and duties of a marketing agent may differ from those of a representative. This ambiguity can lead to a contradiction with the contradicted text, which states that ANUBIS should 'diligently use it best efforts to introduce, promote the sale of, and obtain orders for, the Products in the Sales Territory.' If a 'marketing agent' is not explicitly empowered to 'obtain orders', or if their role is limited to promotion without direct sales, it directly contradicts this obligation. Also, The use of 'patrons/consumers' (plural), creates uncertainty around targeting, contradicting any marketing strategies that are to be implemented. This misalignment in terminology creates a potential in-text contradiction, especially when considering ANUBIS's obligation to obtain orders and effectively contradicting the marketing strategies due to the change in target audience.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 LIMITED WARRANTY. Any products purchased from SCAM which become defective will be Warranted to ANUBIS for a period of 24 months from the date of shipment to ANUBIS. Such warranted units will be repaired or replaced by SCAM.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 LIMITED WARRANTY. Any goods purchased from SCAM which become defective will be Guaranteed to ANUBIS for a period of 24 months from the date of shipment to ANUBIS. Such guaranteed units will be repaired or replaced by SCAM.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Warranted' has been replaced with 'Guaranteed'. A warranty usually has specific conditions and exclusions, while a guarantee often implies a broader, unconditional promise. This change can create uncertainty about the scope and limitations of SCAM's obligation, directly creating an in-text contradiction. Also now there is an in-text contradiction between the title "LIMITED WARRANTY" and the text where any defective goods are "Guaranteed".
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 LIMITED WARRANTY. Any goods purchased from SCAM which become defective will be Guaranteed to ANUBIS for a period of 24 months from the date of shipment to ANUBIS. Such guaranteed units will be repaired or replaced by SCAM.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Warranted' to 'Guaranteed' introduces a contradiction with the title 'LIMITED WARRANTY.' A warranty is typically understood to have specific conditions, while a guarantee often implies a broader promise. This creates uncertainty about the scope and limitations of SCAM's obligation, creating an in-text contradiction because the heading states it's a 'LIMITED WARRANTY' but the text states that the goods are 'Guaranteed' which goes against the idea of a limited warranty. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition, where the use of 'Guaranteed' creates a conflict with 'Limited Warranty'. The Changed Text and Contradicted Text are in the same location.

perturbation.original_text: 5.3 This Agreement will apply to all confidential and proprietary information disclosed by one party to the other party, including information listed in Exhibit A and other information which the disclosing party identifies in writing as confidential before or within thirty days after disclosure to the receiving party ("CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION") 5.4 Each party agrees (i) to hold the other party's Confidential Information in strict confidence, (ii) not to disclose such Confidential Information to any third parties, and (iii) not to use any Confidential Information for
perturbation.changed_text: 5.3 This Agreement will apply to all private and exclusive information disclosed by one party to the other party, including information listed in Exhibit A and other information which the disclosing party identifies in writing as sensitive before or within thirty days after disclosure to the receiving party ("CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION") 5.4 Each party agrees (i) to hold the other party's Confidential Information in high confidence, (ii) not to disclose such Confidential Information to any unauthorized parties, and (iii) not to use any Confidential Information for
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation introduces 'private' and 'exclusive' replacing 'confidential' and 'proprietary', 'sensitive' replacing 'confidential', 'high confidence' replacing 'strict confidence', and 'unauthorized parties' replacing 'third parties'. By changing these, the level of protection, sensitivity and authorization requirements have been modified. A 'third party' can include authorized consultants, creating conflict with the 'unauthorized' term, thereby creating in-text contradictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees (i) to hold the other party's Confidential Information in high confidence, (ii) not to disclose such Confidential Information to any unauthorized parties, and (iii) not to use any Confidential Information for
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'confidential' with 'private' and 'exclusive', 'sensitive' and 'strict confidence' with 'high confidence', and 'third parties' with 'unauthorized parties'. This creates a contradiction because the original text, which the contradicted text is based upon, implies a broader scope of confidentiality and restriction (third parties) than the modified text (unauthorized parties). Specifically, changing 'third parties' to 'unauthorized parties' introduces ambiguity and narrows the scope of restriction, allowing disclosure to authorized third parties which creates a conflict. The explanation points out this can include authorized consultants creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8.2 EVENTS OF DEFAULT. The following shall constitute and Event of Default: (a) The breach by either party of any of the terms of this agreement or of any other ancillary agreement between the parties hereto including, but not limited to, timely payment of any sums due hereunder or thereunder, and such breach continuing for a period of thirty (30) days after notice from the non-breaching party.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.2 EVENTS OF NON-COMPLIANCE. The following shall constitute and Event of Non-Compliance: (a) The violation by either party of any of the terms of this agreement or of any other attached agreement between the parties hereto including, but not limited to, timely payment of any sums due hereunder or thereunder, and such violation continuing for a period of thirty (30) days after notice from the conforming party.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'EVENTS OF DEFAULT' and 'breach' have been replaced with 'EVENTS OF NON-COMPLIANCE' and 'violation,' and 'non-breaching party' has been replaced with 'conforming party.' While seeming similar, 'non-compliance' and 'violation' can imply different levels of severity or intent than 'default' and 'breach.' Replacing 'non-breaching' with 'conforming party' shifts the focus from the act of breaching to an ongoing state of conformity. This change causes uncertainty and potential disputes over what actions constitute a serious enough deviation to trigger termination rights, creating contradictions about how the agreements should be upheld.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Should an Event of Non-Compliance occur the non-defaulting party might, so long as the Event of Non-Compliance continues in effect, terminate this agreement and pursue any right which may be available by law. In the event of any termination arising as a result of a violation by ANUBIS:
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'EVENTS OF DEFAULT' with 'EVENTS OF NON-COMPLIANCE' and 'breach' with 'violation.' The contradicted text references 'Event of Non-Compliance.' While seemingly similar, the explanation states that 'non-compliance' and 'violation' can imply different levels of severity or intent than 'default' and 'breach.' This creates a contradiction because the original text defined default, while the changed text does not, and refers to non-compliance, the original text does not. Furthermore, the Contradicted Text states the actions one can take should an 'Event of Non-Compliance' occur, which makes determining the rights of the party unclear, as the standard for Non-Compliance might be different than Default.
